# Scroll Area

Keeps native scrolling behavior while applying consistent scrollbar styling.

## Installation

Add Scroll Area from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/scroll-area
```

## Usage

Give `ScrollArea` an explicit height, width, or maximum size so overflow can occur. It includes a vertical scrollbar by default and leaves the slotted content in the native scrolling viewport.

```vue
<template>
  <ScrollArea class="h-64 w-72 rounded-md border">
    <div class="p-4">
      <h4 class="mb-4 text-sm font-medium">Release tags</h4>
      <div v-for="tag in tags" :key="tag" class="text-sm">
        {{ tag }}
        <Separator class="my-2" />
      </div>
    </div>
  </ScrollArea>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ScrollArea } from '@/Components/ScrollArea'
import { Separator } from '@/Components/Separator'

const tags = Array.from({ length: 18 }, (_, index) => `v0.${18 - index}.0`)
</script>
```

## Horizontal scrolling

Add `ScrollBar orientation="horizontal"` when content has an intentional inline width. Preserve the content's width with `w-max` or equivalent layout rules; otherwise it may shrink instead of overflowing.

**Horizontal example**

```vue
<template>
  <ScrollArea class="w-full max-w-md whitespace-nowrap rounded-md border">
    <div class="flex w-max gap-4 p-4">
      <div
        v-for="project in projects"
        :key="project"
        class="w-40 rounded-md bg-muted p-4 text-sm font-medium"
      >
        {{ project }}
      </div>
    </div>
    <ScrollBar orientation="horizontal" />
  </ScrollArea>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ScrollArea, ScrollBar } from '@/Components/ScrollArea'

const projects = ['Storefront', 'Documentation', 'Design system', 'Admin']
</script>
```

## Visibility and direction

The root forwards Reka Scroll Area props. Its `type` can be `hover`, `scroll`, `auto`, `always`, or `glimpse`; hover is the local default. `scroll-hide-delay` controls delayed hiding for applicable types, and `dir` sets reading direction when it is not inherited.

## Responsive layout

Avoid nested scrolling regions on small screens, especially when both parent and child scroll in the same direction. Prefer natural document flow for long page content. Use a bounded area for dense sidebars, menus, logs, or media strips whose surrounding layout must remain fixed.

## Accessibility

Keyboard, wheel, touch, and assistive-technology scrolling remain tied to the native viewport. Keep focusable content in a logical order and do not hide critical actions beyond an undiscoverable horizontal scroll. Add a visible label or landmark around the region when its purpose is not obvious from nearby context.

## When to use

- Use Scroll Area when a deliberately bounded region needs themed scrollbars.
- Use ordinary overflow utilities when custom scrollbar behavior adds no value.
- Do not use it merely to shorten content that should remain visible in page flow.

Hosted documentation: [Scroll Area](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/scroll-area)
